title: Statement on IOSCO Compliance for Federal Reserve Bank of New York Administered Reference Rates

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: The Federal Reserve Bank of New York released an updated Statement of Compliance confirming that its administered reference rates meet IOSCO Principles for Financial Benchmarks. The New York Fed's Audit Group conducted an independent review of the governance, quality, methodology, and accountability frameworks for seven reference rates including SOFR, EFFR, and OBFR. The institution will continue annual compliance assessments.
key_points: The New York Fed's Audit Group independently verified compliance with IOSCO Principles for seven reference rates: EFFR, OBFR, TGCR, BGCR, SOFR, SOFR Averages, and SOFR Index
IOSCO published the Principles for Financial Benchmarks in 2013
The Financial Stability Board endorsed these principles as standards of best practice for benchmark administration
Compliance review covered four areas: governance, quality of the benchmark, quality of the methodology, and accountability
The New York Fed commits to annual compliance assessments going forward
why_it_matters: SOFR and other reference rates administered by the New York Fed serve as the foundation for trillions of dollars in derivatives, loans, and securities pricing. Confirmation of IOSCO compliance reinforces market confidence in the integrity and reliability of these critical rate benchmarks, supporting market stability and transparency in rate-based transactions.
market_structure_context: This statement addresses benchmark governance and oversight—core market infrastructure elements. IOSCO Principles establish standards for benchmark administrators covering conflict management, methodology quality, and accountability mechanisms. The annual compliance commitment provides ongoing assurance about reference rate integrity.
notes: Source is a formal compliance statement from a high-trust domain (Federal Reserve Bank of New York). The document is factual and contains specific details about which rates were reviewed and what IOSCO principles were assessed. No specific market-moving data or forward-looking statements present; this is a procedural/regulatory compliance update. URL field is blank in source metadata.
